title: Disability Related Transportation - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Transportation > Disability Related Transportation 77 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BARBARA ROSS BUILDING DEVELOPMENTAL DIVERSITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Services can include case management; individual and family support plans; access to brokerage services for transportation; in-home supports or residential placement; and employment and vocational services. 4185 SW Research Way Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals with an intellectual or developmental disability. Applicant must be an Oregon resident and a U.S. citizen. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 962-8800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management and helps access available services and resources to eligible individuals who experience Intellectual and/or Developmental Disabilities. Service coordinators connect individuals to services which may include in-home support, residential services, supported living, employment, alternatives to employment, transportation, protective services, and other community resources and benefits. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Children and adults with an Intellectual and/or Developmental Disabilities who meet the eligibility criteria. Applicants must be an Oregon resident. Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm Email (503) 557-2824 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation for medical appointments, personal business, and shopping. Priority is given to life-sustaining medical appointments. Rides are provided within Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties. The Ride Together program allows riders to recruit their own volunteer driver (i.e., family, neighbors, and friends). Participants schedule rides with their volunteer driver. Mileage reimbursement is provided, and volunteer training is required. The Vets Driving Vets program matches veterans who volunteer their services with other veterans who need rides to doctors' appointments or to take care of other basic needs. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Age 65 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Transportation services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 655-8208 3 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE–MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, transportation, and meal preparation and delivery. Adult day services (non-residential community-based programs, providing services for part of the day). Registered nurse services (one-time or intermittent assessment, service planning, provider training, and education). Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences). Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. In-home service hours are available up to 20 hours per week, based on individual need. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Adults, age 18 and older, who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the Federal Poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in their own home and not a provider-operator or owned facility or foster/care home Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm (503) 655-8640 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP BEHAVIORAL HEALTHCARE DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Services may include case management, individual and family support plans, access to brokerage services for transportation, in-home supports or residential placement,and employment and vocational services. 115 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals with an intellectual or developmental disability. Applicant must be an Oregon resident and a U.S. citizen. Provides additional support services like job finding assistance, Medicare information and counseling, transportation to medical appointments, emergency financial grants, support groups, volunteer opportunities, advocacy, and more. 100 SW Main Street Room 212a Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Veterans or dependents of veterans, with a focus on those with disabilities Hours: -National Call Center: 6am-1pm -Local Office Phone: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-3:30pm, Wednesday 8am-noon -Local Office Walk in: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-2:30pm, Wednesday 8am-noon Email (503) 412-4750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GILLIAM COUNTY SENIOR SERVICES GILLIAM COUNTY TRANSPORTATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportion to medical appointments, shopping, social, or business trips, with medical being the priority. 615 E Cottonwood Lane Condon, OR 97823 Eligibility: Unrestricted; age 60 and older and people with disabilities are prioritized Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Transportation available Monday-Friday, and occasional weekends Email (541) 351-9545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. Participants may receive up to 20 hours of caregiving assistance per week. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 5240 NE Elam Young Parkway Suite 300 Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Adults, age 18 and older, who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the federal poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in their own home and not a provider-operator or owned facility or foster/care home Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-3060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH YAMHILL COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES SERVICES COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Services can include case management; individual and family support plans; access to brokerage services for transportation; in-home supports or residential placement; and employment and vocational services. 330 NE Kirby Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Individuals with an intellectual or developmental disability. Applicants must be an Oregon resident and a US citizen. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 434-7460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DEVELOPMENTAL DIVERSITY SERVICES BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES JEFFERSON COUNTY
